title: Password reset and account sign-in issues
steps: Use the LEC self-service password reset portal and complete identity verification.
Create a new password with at least 12 characters, uppercase, lowercase, number, and symbol.
Wait 2-3 minutes for directory sync, then sign out and sign in again.
If account is locked, wait 15 minutes or ask service desk to unlock the account.
escalation: Escalate if reset fails 3 times, MFA is unavailable, or account remains locked after 15 minutes.

title: MFA or authenticator problems
steps: Check the phone time is set to automatic date/time.
Open authenticator app and refresh the one-time code.
Try alternate method (SMS/call) if configured.
If you changed phone, request MFA re-registration from service desk.
escalation: Escalate immediately if no backup MFA method exists and access is blocked.

title: VPN connection failure
steps: Confirm internet is stable by opening public websites.
Disconnect and reconnect VPN client, then retry login.
Restart VPN client service or reboot laptop.
Ensure your password was recently updated in VPN credentials.
escalation: Escalate if error persists for 10+ minutes or multiple users in one team are impacted.

title: Wi-Fi unstable or disconnected
steps: Toggle Wi-Fi off/on and reconnect to the approved SSID.
Forget network and reconnect using current credentials.
Move closer to access point or use wired ethernet if available.
Run Windows network troubleshooter and renew IP.
escalation: Escalate if same location has repeated drops or multiple users report the issue.

title: Slow network performance
steps: Run a speed test and capture screenshot of results.
Close heavy background apps (cloud sync, updates, streaming).
Switch from Wi-Fi to ethernet for validation.
Retry critical business app and compare response time.
escalation: Escalate with speed-test result and affected application names if SLA impact is observed.

title: Outlook not sending or receiving email
steps: Check if Outlook is in Offline mode and disable it.
Restart Outlook and run Send/Receive all folders.
Verify mailbox quota is not full.
Create a new Outlook profile if issue is profile-related.
escalation: Escalate if multiple users in same domain are affected or mail flow is delayed for more than 30 minutes.

title: Microsoft Teams call/audio issue
steps: Check Teams device settings and select correct microphone/speaker.
Close other apps using audio device (Zoom, browser tabs, media players).
Run Teams test call and confirm permissions for microphone/camera.
Sign out and sign in to Teams, then retry.
escalation: Escalate if device tests pass but audio still fails in corporate meetings.

title: Printer offline
steps: Power cycle printer and check network cable or Wi-Fi status.
On PC, remove stuck print jobs and restart Print Spooler service.
Set printer as default and print test page.
Reinstall printer driver if status remains offline.
escalation: Escalate if printer is shared and multiple users cannot print.

title: Printer paper jam
steps: Open all jam access doors and remove paper carefully in feed direction.
Check rollers and tray alignment, then reload paper stack properly.
Clear printer queue and retry one-page test print.
If sensor still reports jam, clean internal paper path.
escalation: Escalate for onsite hardware check if jam error remains after full clearing.

title: Toner/cartridge replacement
steps: Check toner level from printer panel.
Remove and reseat cartridge, then print test page.
Replace cartridge with approved stock item.
Update consumables inventory after replacement.
escalation: Escalate if print quality remains poor with a new cartridge.

title: Laptop not powering on
steps: Connect charger directly to wall power and confirm charging LED.
Hold power button for 15 seconds, then restart.
Disconnect dock/peripherals and boot with only charger attached.
If available, test with known working charger.
escalation: Escalate immediately for hardware diagnostics if no power indicators appear.

title: Blue screen or frequent crashes
steps: Capture stop code and recent changes (driver/app updates).
Boot in Safe Mode and uninstall latest problematic update.
Run memory diagnostics and disk check.
Install latest approved chipset and OS updates.
escalation: Escalate if crash repeats more than twice per day or affects production work.

title: Software installation request/failure
steps: Confirm software is approved by IT policy and license is available.
Run installer as administrator and disable conflicting apps.
Check endpoint security blocks or missing prerequisites (.NET/VC++).
Retry installation after reboot.
escalation: Escalate with installer logs and screenshot of exact error code.

title: Shared drive not accessible
steps: Verify VPN/corporate network is connected.
Test access via UNC path (example: \\server\share).
Remap drive and re-enter credentials if prompted.
Confirm user has correct access rights.
escalation: Escalate if access rights are correct but share is unavailable to multiple users.

title: Remote desktop (RDP) connection issue
steps: Confirm target machine is powered on and reachable on VPN.
Use hostname and IP to test both connection methods.
Check firewall and RDP service status on target machine.
Clear saved credentials and reconnect.
escalation: Escalate if target host is reachable but RDP port/service remains blocked.

title: Browser issues in corporate web apps
steps: Clear browser cache and cookies for affected site.
Try private/incognito mode and disable extensions.
Test on alternate supported browser.
Confirm date/time is correct and TLS certificate is valid.
escalation: Escalate with URL, timestamp, browser version, and screenshot if issue persists.

title: Antivirus alert or endpoint protection block
steps: Do not bypass or disable antivirus policy.
Run full endpoint scan and isolate suspicious file.
Capture threat name, path, and event timestamp.
If business app is blocked, submit false-positive review request.
escalation: Escalate immediately for suspected malware or repeated endpoint alerts.

title: Phishing or suspicious email
steps: Do not click links or open attachments.
Report email using phishing-report tool in mail client.
Forward header details to security team if required.
Delete the message after reporting.
escalation: Escalate as high priority if credentials were entered or attachment executed.

title: Windows update failure
steps: Ensure minimum 10GB free disk space.
Restart device and retry update.
Run Windows Update troubleshooter.
Install required cumulative update manually if guided by IT.
escalation: Escalate if security patch remains missing after two retries.

title: Low disk space
steps: Delete temp files and empty recycle bin.
Move large files to approved shared storage.
Uninstall unused applications.
Run disk cleanup and restart.
escalation: Escalate if free space remains below 10% after cleanup.

title: Keyboard or mouse not responding
steps: Reconnect keyboard/mouse and test another USB port.
Restart the device and check if input works before login.
In Device Manager, uninstall and re-detect keyboard/mouse drivers.
If wireless, replace batteries and re-pair the device.
escalation: Escalate if issue persists with known good keyboard/mouse on the same workstation.

title: Application freezing or not responding
steps: Close the application from Task Manager and reopen it.
Restart the workstation and retry the same action.
Clear app cache/temp files if the app supports it.
Capture screenshot/error details and test with another user profile if possible.
escalation: Escalate with app name, version, and exact action that causes the freeze.

title: Connected to network but no internet access
steps: Disconnect/reconnect network adapter and confirm valid IP address.
Flush DNS cache and renew IP configuration.
Test gateway and DNS reachability with ping/nslookup.
Try ethernet if on Wi-Fi to isolate access-point issues.
escalation: Escalate if multiple users at same branch are connected but unable to access internet services.

title: Docking station or external monitor issue
steps: Reconnect dock power and cable connections.
Press Win+P and select correct display mode.
Update display/dock drivers.
Test with a different cable/port.
escalation: Escalate if monitor works on another device but not on assigned laptop.
